what_it_does: This file is a posted noticeboard note authored by Tim that records his reaction to a prior UI interaction proposal (a press-and-slide level picker with stepped detents and haptic ticks). He explains that he cannot evaluate the idea from a text description alone, asks for interactive mockups in the future, and argues that this feedback must be stored as persistent, agent-accessible company memory rather than remaining in the ephemeral context of the agents involved.
observations: The note is a comment on a prior board item (item-0973c052) about a stepped level picker with haptic feedback.
Tim states he does not intuitively understand the described interaction and cannot imagine it without a working prototype.
He requests future design proposals be delivered as interactive mockups he can try and react to.
He suggests such mockups might reuse mechanisms from the annotation systems currently being built, calling them a 'generative proposal'.
He frames capturing user feedback as a core reason for building the company and its memory infrastructure.
He asserts he is the sole user, origin, and source of requirements.
The file contains no code symbols.
standouts: The note quickly moves from a specific UI reaction to a meta-methodology request for interactive, generative mockups.
It strongly emphasizes that feedback must not be trapped in the 'living memory' of participating agents.
It is unusually candid about the limits of verbal design discussion for this user.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item schema with YAML frontmatter including id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history.
Uses a markdown body with a [point] marker and a quoted excerpt from the prior proposal.
Breaks from terse technical notes by being a long, stream-of-consciousness user feedback monologue.
concerns: The note mixes several threads—UI evaluation, future tooling desire, company philosophy, and identity rhetoric—which may make it harder to action or retrieve.
It does not define concrete next steps, owners, or acceptance criteria for the requested mockups.
Some phrasing ('I am everything that is not everything else') is semantically ambiguous for downstream agents.
notes: Tim's feedback on a haptic level-picker proposal, pivoting to a request for interactive mockups and persistent agent-accessible memory.
questions: What is the full content of the prior proposal at item-0973c052 beyond the quoted excerpt?
What does 'generative proposal' specifically mean in the current tooling context?
Which annotation systems is Tim referring to as the basis for mockup delivery?
What is the intended retrieval path so that future agents automatically become 'aware' of this feedback?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose is a simple point/note; the actual content is a multi-layered reaction, future-feature request, and memory-system philosophy, so it exceeds a simple point.
apparent_themes: Interactive prototyping over verbal design review
Persistent organizational memory versus ephemeral agent context
User-as-origin and sole arbiter of requirements
Honest acknowledgment of platform limitations (iOS Safari haptics)
intention_signals: Tim wants the team/agents to stop asking for verbal reactions to abstract UI descriptions and instead produce tryable interactive mockups, while also insisting that this very feedback be encoded into durable company memory so future agents honor it automatically.
novelty: It explicitly articulates a user-feedback-as-infrastructure requirement: the response is not only about the UI idea but about how all future ideas should be presented, evaluated, and remembered.
contribution: Captures a user-level design-review policy (interactive mockups required for meaningful feedback) and a memory-system requirement (feedback must be persistent and agent-accessible), both of which a fused system should retain as constraints for future design proposals and agent behavior.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard note by Tim responding to a stepped haptic level-picker proposal, stating he cannot evaluate it without interactive mockups and asking that such feedback be stored as persistent, agent-accessible company memory.
intention_for_embedding: To redirect future design communication toward tryable generative prototypes and to ensure this user feedback becomes durable organizational context for all agents.
